dialogs.greeting: Ah, you're awake. We have a situation.

nodes.speaker: Captain
nodes.text: Ah, you're awake. We have a situation.

nodes.speaker: Captain
nodes.text: The warp core took a hit during the storm. We're drifting in uncharted space. I need you to get us back online.

choices.text: What do I need to do?

nodes.speaker: Captain
nodes.text: Somewhere in the Outer Rim. The nav computer is offline. Check the observation deck telescope — it might help us triangulate our position.

nodes.speaker: Captain
nodes.text: Engineer Chen left her tools in the engine room. You'll need a hydro spanner and a replacement coil. Good luck.

nodes.speaker: Captain
nodes.text: The comms array is down too. If you find a comms beacon, you can rig one from the viewscreen. Range won't be great, but it's something.

nodes.speaker: Captain
nodes.text: Priority one: repair the warp core. Priority two: send a distress signal. Check the crew quarters for anything useful. Dismissed.

dialogs.greeting: Oh, thank goodness. I've been stuck down here for hours.

nodes.speaker: Engineer Chen
nodes.text: Oh, thank goodness. I've been stuck down here for hours.

nodes.speaker: Engineer Chen
nodes.text: Warp core is fried. I managed to salvage a replacement coil, but I need my hydro spanner to install it. I think I left it on the engineering console.

nodes.speaker: Engineer Chen
nodes.text: Just bruised. The core breach knocked me out. Listen — the fuel gauge needs calibration too. Use my spanner: HIGH, LOW, MEDIUM. That's the sequence.

nodes.speaker: Engineer Chen
nodes.text: Also, the wall panel in the corridor has a security code. If you ever need engineering override, the sequence is ALPHA, GAMMA, BETA. Don't ask how I know.

nodes.speaker: Engineer Chen
nodes.text: Fuel's running low. The gauge is off — you'll need to recalibrate it. My spanner should do the trick.

dialogs.greeting: Fascinating. The stellar readings from this position are unlike anything I've seen.

nodes.speaker: Dr. Vega
nodes.text: Fascinating. The stellar readings from this position are unlike anything I've seen.

nodes.speaker: Dr. Vega
nodes.text: The star chart on the observation deck might help. I found a fragment that could complete it. Look near the telescope.

nodes.speaker: Dr. Vega
nodes.text: I've been cataloging everything in the crew quarters. There's a data chip with encrypted logs — might be worth decoding on the personal terminal.

nodes.speaker: Dr. Vega
nodes.text: The telescope needs its lens cap. Without it, the readings are useless. Once you align it with the star map, we should get coordinates.

nodes.speaker: Dr. Vega
nodes.text: The captain is on the bridge, Engineer Chen is in the engine room. I've been up here cataloging. We're all accounted for — for now.
